stories.headline: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin

stories.headline: Global steel crisis deepens as oversupply reaches alarming levels, OECD warns
stories.summary: The global steel industry is facing a growing crisis as subsidised production and excess capacity continue to distort markets, according to the OECD. It says state-backed steel production, particularly in China, is increasing pressure on producers in Europe and other OECD economies.

stories.headline: Sempra Unit Starts Producing LNG at New Export Plant in Mexico
stories.summary: Sempra Infrastructure said it began producing liquefied natural gas from Mexico’s first West Coast export terminal, with plans to ultimately ship the fuel to Asia in a move that could add relief to a market hurt by tightened global supply due to the Iran war.

stories.headline: Wall Street Sees SpaceX’s AI Revenue 100 Times Higher by 2030
stories.summary: Research analysts across Wall Street are telling would-be SpaceX IPO buyers that they’re modeling for the company’s artificial intelligence division to see 100 times revenue growth at the end of the decade, to help justify a targeted $1.8 trillion valuation.

stories.headline: Saylor’s Bitcoin Machine Is Misfiring on Every Cylinder
stories.summary: Michael Saylor built Strategy Inc. around a straightforward idea: raise money to buy Bitcoin, then never sell it. Over time, that simple bet has turned far more complicated.

stories.headline: Princeton Endowment Backs Out of Oil and Gas Divestment Vow
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Janet Lorin joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Princeton University’s endowment is backtracking on its pledge to divest from publicly traded oil and gas companies, four years after it said it would exit such holdings as way to move toward a net-zero portfolio. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Postal Service Frees Up Enough Cash to Keep Operating for ‘Several Years’
stories.summary: The agency had warned it could run out of money within months, but its regulators said suspending payments to a retirement fund had given officials more time to find a permanent solution.

stories.headline: Disney racks up $4.2bn deficit on Paris parks
